item.title: Unique
item.content:encoded:
I appreciated the originality and simplicity of this. It's refreshing to be able to find new ideas within thousands of articles.

Thank you.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image is a modified painting of artist Gilbert Munger (deceased as of 1903), whose works are in the public domain and CC-compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this SCP is really good, but for me, it raises a big question- how exactly do people with BASE jumping gear survive? Does a parachute function as if the person was dropping despite a lack of actual movement? It's very hard for me to picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are no mistakes, just happy little accidents! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you mean people wearing BASE jumping suit will survive the "drop" even though they don't utilize it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand why exactly but you just earned this SCP my 2nd favorite SCP. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of all the random scips that are insta-kills, this one really appeals to the adrenalin junkie in me. I'd want it simply for walking around in BASE jumping gear! And as someone who avoids stairs like the plague due to a knee injury, I think I may fair alright with this one.

All that aside, +1 from me. Is rather well done.
yur skip just fly!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the simplicity of this one— sometimes it's just the tiny details that elevate a simple article from dull to enigmatic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, there's room for expansion on this, but it's good anyway. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what would happen if the effect triggered while you were on an escalator… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perhaps, but if this is a 'magic death painting', they're still just 'magic death X's.'. If the whole "Percieving all drops as deadly cliff faces and having them act like such towards the perciever' doesn't grab you, that's fine, buti t's still not just a painting that kills you because you looked at it.

(As opposed to a statue of a death goddess we have, which DOES just kill you if you've looked at it.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hope they see the same cliff height every time, even if they're looking at the earth from the moon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fuck you and your probably-not-living-in-a-basement-ness. And your +1-ness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So what happens if a fully affected subject looks down a 4000 m drop? out an airplane?

How's this thing work if you're in orbit?

Does the effect extend to the vehicle you're in?

In the example of stairs, what does the subject see if they slide down the railing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some scps make death interesting, some don't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a Loootaaaa SCP's on this site you're going to dislike, then.

We've also got magic death lizards, and magic death statues,a nd magic-death-guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you cut the figure that would show up and push people off the 'cliffs' if they refused to jump, but I'm just not engaged by what amounts to a magic death painting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno, I'm a bit torn on this. It's an interesting concept, and I like the idea of turning even the tiniest of drops into a perceived massive drop, but…

hover in the air for approximately thirty seconds before impacting the ground with force comparable to that expected of striking the ground at terminal velocity.

I can't help but imagining something akin to Looney Toons when I read that, and I don't think that's a good thing to have happen for a serious article.

No vote for now, I'll probably wander back to this a little later and upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's this? Interesting anart that isn't AWCY? Is that a +1 I hear calling?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The painting thing is a little odd with the "trigger phrase" thing that isn't mentioned in the rest of the article. You'd think they'd just blockbox it or something if that's so important. Also the note is really ???, since it doesn't tell us anything the article doesn't tell us other than this "sky is calling", which isn't mentioned anywhere else in the article. Kind of like, I guess theres something subtle happening and I'm not seeing it. Maybe it's just because I just woke up. Maybe it's because I haven't gotten enough sleep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much appreciated, and I agree. Someone reviewed it and mentioned that in chat earlier; I'll be fixing it up tomorrow (on a Chromebook now, so that's kind of a placeholder).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the picture should have a big black box in it, or the caption should mention it's been cropped. I can't imagine Foundation personnel taking the time to lovingly Photoshop a memetic agent out of a picture so it looks unedited.

But that's nitpicking. The SCP itself works very well, it's simple but effective, and it's earned my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not actually the case. This is my third attempt, but the first time I tried running it by people first. Live and learn :)

And thanks for the positive comments! I tweaked the addendum a little bit; after further thought, I agree it was a bit cheesy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Situations like this are why I have the keys to an orbital ion lance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this. This is a great example of how a persons first SCP will not always fail. Great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the whole thing. Combining the effective imagery of someone stepping over a stair, floating for a bit, and then bodyslamming the ground with the force of a thousand suns with the wonder of what this guy was thinking, where the painting came from, and why he decided to end it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short, sweet, and overall enjoyable. Felt the addendum was a bit cheesy, though. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The addendum was meh, but I liked the concept well enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoy this. Simple, effective, bold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Simple, but oddly I kind of like it.

onasidenotepleasedon'tkillmemackenzie
